title: no intermediate wait for continue
purpose: A2.7 defines no intermediate wait for continue for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A2.7` only; siblings under `A2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: With autopilot.active true, the conductor runs the [A2.6](A2.6-loop-until-blocked-budget-achieved-h3-reject.md) canonical loop—[A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight, one [A2.2](A2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) phase, [A2.3](A2.3-post-step-route-tier-dual-write-increment.md) post-step—back-to-back in the same session without prompting the operator to type "continue" between skill phases.
Manual `/continue` or operator "continue" executes exactly one pipeline step only when autopilot is inactive; it does not babysit each phase inside an active pursuit loop.

Permitted human pauses are H1 plan approval, H2 blocker resolution, and H3 final sign-off per [INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md); check-pipeline-blocked BLOCKED exits the loop until H2 clears.
Skills, rules, and run-local-pipeline must not reintroduce intermediate wait-for-user gates between post-step dual-write and the next preflight except at H1, H2, or H3 touchpoints.

If a conductor turn ends while autopilot remains active without advancing pursuit or dual-writing an H2 stop reason, the next wake fails closed at preflight BLOCKED until journal and state.json reconcile the stall.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A2.7` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A2.7`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A2.7` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A2.7`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A2.7 defines no intermediate wait for continue for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system with autopilot.active true, the conductor runs the [a2.6](a2.6-loop-until-blocked-budget-achieved-h3-reject.md) canonical loop—[a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) preflight, one [a2.2](a2.2-if-ready-execute-one-pipeline-step.md) phase, [a2.3](a2.3-post-step-route-tier-dual-write-increment.md) post-step—back-to-back in the same session without prompting the operator to type "continue" between skill phases, then manual `/continue` or operator "continue" executes exactly one pipeline step only when autopilot is inactive; it does not babysit each phase inside an active pursuit loop.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent pilot 2026-06-28 -->

Earlier automation models asked humans to type “continue” after every step. This capability forbids intermediate wait-for-continue: the loop runs autonomously between H1, H2, and H3 touchpoints.

“Continue” in operator language now means one pipeline step when manually invoked—not a prompt between every phase inside autopilot. That redefinition is what makes 100% automation between touchpoints achievable.

Confusion here causes products to re-introduce manual babysitting. This spec is the guardrail against regressing to burst-and-wait workflows.
